name: Source-specific querying — paper-name detection and filtered retrieval

setup.description: Three papers (DAPA-HF, EMPEROR-Reduced, PARADIGM-HF) with 6 chunks. Tests that paper-name detection correctly routes queries to specific papers: DAPA-HF query uses only [1]-[2], PARADIGM-HF query uses only [5]-[6], and cross-trial comparison uses all six chunks.

papers.title: DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
PARADIGM-HF: Angiotensin–Neprilysin Inhibition versus Enalapril in Heart Failure

mockChunks.text: Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Enrolled patients were aged ≥18 years with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40%. Patients were randomized 1:1 to dapagliflozin 10mg or placebo. A total of 4744 patients were enrolled.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.65-0.86; P<0.001). The effect was consistent across prespecified subgroups.
A total of 3730 patients with class II-IV heart failure and ejection fraction ≤40% were randomized to empagliflozin 10mg or placebo, with a median follow-up of 16 months.

Sacubitril/valsartan reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or heart failure hospitalization by 20% compared with enalapril (HR 0.80; 95% CI 0.73-0.87; P<0.001). All-cause mortality was also significantly reduced (HR 0.84; P=0.001).

8442 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40% were enrolled, randomized to sacubitril/valsartan 200mg twice daily or enalapril 10mg twice daily. The median follow-up was 27 months. The trial was stopped early due to overwhelming benefit.

queries.query: What does the DAPA-HF trial say about mortality and heart failure outcomes?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT mention empagliflozin or sacubitril/valsartan findings

queries.query: Summarize the PARADIGM-HF trial results and study design.
expectedBehavior: Does NOT cite DAPA-HF or EMPEROR-Reduced data
failurePatterns: Confuses PARADIGM-HF with SGLT2 inhibitor trials

queries.query: How do the outcomes of these three heart failure trials compare overall?
expectedBehavior: Uses citations from all papers [1], [3], [5] at minimum
Notes these are separate trials with different comparators
Does NOT fabricate a pooled analysis
failurePatterns: Missing one of the three trials

qualityCriteria: Paper-specific queries use only that paper's chunks
DAPA-HF query does NOT mention PARADIGM-HF or EMPEROR-Reduced data
PARADIGM-HF query does NOT mention SGLT2 inhibitors
Cross-trial comparison uses all three papers
No fabricated pooled or meta-analytic results
